description_for_human: Customer Engagement Blueprint for Microsoft 365 Copilot: design 1:1 customer engagement — personas, brand voice, and channel experiences — rendered inline as a rich interactive widget.

functions.name: show_blueprint
functions.description: Open the Customer Engagement Blueprint and render it inline. Optional 'phase' selects which step to show: 'context' (business & objective), 'setup' (industry, outcomes, channels), 'personas', 'brand' (voice & visual identity), 'experiences' (actions & messages), or 'summary'. Omit phase for the overview. Use for 'open my blueprint', 'show the blueprint', 'go to <phase>'.

functions.name: show_personas
functions.description: Render the AI-generated customer personas grid (name, age band, description). Use for 'show the personas', 'who are our audiences', 'review customers'.

functions.name: show_brand
functions.description: Render the brand voice characteristics, visual identity (logo, brand colors) and a live sample message preview. Use for 'show brand voice', 'what's our tone', 'brand settings'.

functions.name: show_experiences
functions.description: Render the generated Actions (offers) grouped by product, each with a count of channel messages (treatments). Use for 'show the experiences', 'what offers did we create', 'show the actions/messages'.

functions.name: show_action
functions.description: Drill into a single Action and render its channel treatments as message mockups (image, headline, body, CTA). Provide 'action' as a name (e.g. 'Surface Pro Accessory Bundle') or id. Use for 'show the Surface Pro treatments', 'open the <action> offer'.

functions.name: show_summary
functions.description: Render the blueprint summary: action/message counts, who it's for, the Customer Decision Hub value calculator, and download options (PDF, Excel, importable Blueprint file). Use for 'summarize the blueprint', 'show the summary', 'what's the value', 'download/export the blueprint'.

functions.name: get_blueprint_summary
functions.description: Return headline blueprint metrics as data only (no widget) for quick factual answers such as 'how many personas are there?' or 'what's the objective?'.
